stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {s : String}, Γ ⇒ #s → Γ ≠ [#s] → (∃ L B A Δ Λ, Γ = L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ ∧ Δ ⇒ A ∧ L ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ #s) ∨ (∃ Γ₁ Δ A B Λ, Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ Λ ∧ Δ ⇒ A ∧ Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ #s)
priority_rationale: Already subsumed by the solved atomic decomposition theorem.

stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (A ⧹ B) → Δ ⇒ A → Δ ++ Γ ⇒ B
priority_rationale: A standard local slash application lemma with little effect on the present frontier.

stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) → Δ ⇒ A → Γ ++ Δ ⇒ B
priority_rationale: Mirror local slash application with little structural payoff now.

stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (A ⧹ B) → Δ ⇒ (B ⧹ C) → Γ ++ Δ ⇒ (A ⧹ C)
priority_rationale: A local compositional corollary rather than a summary-changing bridge.

stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (C ⧸ B) → Δ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) → Γ ++ Δ ⇒ (C ⧸ A)
priority_rationale: Mirror compositional corollary that does not address a live bottleneck.

stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → Γ ⇒ A → is_atom A ∧ Γ = [A]
priority_rationale: This rigidity line is already broken by verified counterexample evidence.

stmt: ∀ {A : Tp} {s : String}, [A] ⇒ Tp.atom s ↔ A = Tp.atom s
summary_delta: The counterexample shows singleton rigidity fails in general, so the first sharp salvage is to ask whether it becomes exact again at atomic conclusions. This is a genuine boundary statement, not a cosmetic restriction, and it would connect the new failure directly to the existing atomic-generation phenomenon.
priority_rationale: Already verified in the current theory.

stmt: ∀ s : String, ∃ A : Tp, ¬ is_atom A ∧ [Tp.atom s] ⇒ A
summary_delta: This turns the single counterexample into a uniform separation theorem: even the smallest purely atomic context can derive a compound type. That is the sharp converse-failure lesson exposed by the refutation, and it rules out any attempt to recover conclusion-atomicity without adding a real extra hypothesis.
priority_rationale: A uniform counterexample family adds little beyond the established singleton-rigidity failure.

summary_delta: The verified counterexample kills one-way singleton rigidity, but it does not yet say whether equality is still exactly the symmetric boundary of singleton interderivability. Resolving this would distinguish a mere one-sided expansion phenomenon from a genuine collapse of the singleton derivability preorder, which is a sharper and more reusable structural lesson.
priority_rationale: Another singleton-rigidity boundary variant without new subsystem or search content.

stmt: Every derivation [A] ⇒ B admits a finite focused singleton decomposition tree obtained by iterating the outermost slash classification on the succedent until the distinguished singleton branch reaches A, with each step recording the corresponding side sequent generated by that slash.
summary_delta: This is the missing bridge from the newly solved one-step singleton split to a global proof-search object. It would explain exactly how singleton derivations grow, make the surviving structure behind failed singleton rigidity explicit, and provide a reusable normal-form interface for later fragment and grammar-facing classifications.
priority_rationale: Singleton-specific decomposition is now too narrow relative to the fragment-internalization agenda.

stmt: ∀ {A B : Tp}, [A] ⇒ B → tp_degree A ≤ tp_degree B, and if A ≠ B then tp_degree A < tp_degree B.
summary_delta: This would turn the singleton classification into a sharp separation invariant rather than just an existence description. A strict degree boundary would rule out nontrivial cycles in singleton derivability, strengthen the surviving rigidity boundary after the known counterexample, and supply a reusable obstruction for later normalization and fragment arguments.
priority_rationale: A quantitative singleton invariant is off the main structural frontier.

stmt: If (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) and Γ ⇒ A, then for some s : String one has Γ = [Tp.atom s], and the derivation of [Tp.atom s] ⇒ A can be put into a form built from the axiom by slash-right introductions only.
summary_delta: This would convert the counterexample pattern into an explicit normal-form theorem for proof search on atomic data. It supplies the missing bridge from the primitive sequent rules to a reusable decomposition principle, which is exactly the frontier the current theory state says is missing.
priority_rationale: Atomic-antecedent normal form is narrower than current one-sided interface goals.

stmt: If every member of Γ is atomic, then Γ ⇒ A if and only if there exists a finite recursive focused tree whose frontier is exactly Γ and whose root formula is A.
summary_delta: The verified counterexample shows that the failed theorem should be salvaged by a positive structural criterion, not by another singleton claim. An exact tree characterization would turn the exposed failure into the missing canonical normal form for all-atomic antecedents and would connect this boundary directly to the active focusing agenda.
priority_rationale: All-atomic focused-tree characterization is positive but no longer central to the theory frontier.

stmt: ∀ k : Nat, ∃ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), list_degree Γ + tp_degree A + 1 = k + 1 ∧ FocusedTree (k + 1) Γ A ∧ ¬ FocusedTree k Γ A
summary_delta: If the canonical degree bound is to matter structurally, it should be shown sharp rather than merely sufficient. A strict separation between successive focus depths would expose a real boundary phenomenon in the theory, showing that the new focusing correspondence is not just existential but carries nontrivial quantitative content about proof-search depth.
priority_rationale: Pure focused-depth sharpness remains overexplored after explicit failures of the sharpness program.

stmt: ∀ N : Nat, ∃ Γ : List Tp, ∃ A : Tp, Γ ⇒ A ∧ FocusedTree N Γ A ∧ ∀ m < N, ¬ FocusedTree m Γ A
summary_delta: After the arbitrary-succedent exactness theorem, focused height becomes a genuine structural parameter rather than an implementation artifact. Proving that minimal focused height is unbounded would yield a real boundary result for the recursive focusing program and show that no fixed small-depth regime captures all derivations.
priority_rationale: Another quantitative depth-separation problem with low leverage on the current summary.

stmt: ∀ n : Nat, ∃ Γ A, AtomicCtx Γ ∧ Γ ⇒ A ∧ ¬ LeftRep Γ A ∧ ¬ RightRep Γ A ∧ n ≤ tp_degree A
summary_delta: The verified result currently gives only one witness. An unbounded family would turn that example into a genuine regime-separation theorem, showing that the gap between the full calculus and the one-sided fragments is not a small-degree accident but persists at arbitrarily high formula complexity.
priority_rationale: An unbounded gap family is quantitative and does not resolve a live structural bottleneck.

stmt: Determine the exact maximum possible value of `length (candidates Γ)` as a function of `length Γ`, and characterize the contexts `Γ` that attain that maximum.
summary_delta: Now that `candidates Γ` is known to be the exact atomic branching frontier, its size is no longer a list-level implementation detail but a sharp proof-search complexity invariant. An extremal theorem here would give a genuine boundary result for atomic search branching and connect the solved exactness theorem to the complexity-oriented agenda.
priority_rationale: Candidate-count extremality is complexity-flavored but peripheral to subsystem internalization.

stmt: Characterize exactly those contexts `Γ` for which every derivable atomic sequent `Γ ⇒ # s` has at most one realizing witness `c ∈ candidates Γ` in the exact classification theorem.
summary_delta: The solved result exposes candidate multiplicity as the first precise notion of atomic proof-search ambiguity. An exact uniqueness criterion would separate deterministic from genuinely branching atomic search, which is structurally reusable for parsing, normalization, and later fragment-separation arguments rather than being just another local corollary.
priority_rationale: Search-ambiguity structure is interesting, but it does not currently bridge fragment predicates or search exactness.

stmt: ∀ N : Nat, (∃ Γ : List Tp, ∃ A : Tp, list_degree Γ + tp_degree A = N ∧ Γ ⇒ A) ↔ 2 ≤ N
summary_delta: The counterexample exposes an exact low-degree obstruction: derivable sequents cannot occur below total degree 2. Turning that obstruction into a full iff theorem gives a clean boundary statement for which total degrees are genuinely realizable, replacing the failed universal claim with an exact threshold rather than a one-off negative instance.
priority_rationale: A realizable-degree threshold is quantitative and detached from the present bottlenecks.

stmt: Let μ(Γ, A) be the least m such that FocusedTree m Γ A. Give an exact recursive characterization of μ that follows the slash-invertible cases for non-atomic succedents and the exact last-step candidate decomposition for atomic succedents.
summary_delta: This would convert bounded focused completeness into a canonical minimal-depth invariant, aligning proof search, focusing, and sequent structure at a finer level than the current upper bound. It is a reusable bridge from the solved threshold theorem to exact quantitative control, and it would clarify how the search space is structurally built rather than merely bounded.
priority_rationale: Minimal focused depth is a refinement of the already down-ranked quantitative program.

stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, Γ ⇒ A → (list_degree Γ + tp_degree A = 2 ∨ 5 ≤ list_degree Γ + tp_degree A)
summary_delta: The counterexample shows that the universal sharpness claim already fails at the smallest derivable size. A theorem ruling out total degrees 3 and 4 would turn that into a genuine boundary result: degree 2 would be the isolated atomic base case, and every nontrivial derivable sequent would start only at degree 5. That materially sharpens the realizability frontier needed for any corrected focused-depth sharpness theorem.
priority_rationale: Another degree-gap refinement in a currently low-yield direction.

stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (Γ ⇒ A ∧ list_degree Γ + tp_degree A = 2) ↔ ∃ s : String, Γ = [Tp.atom s] ∧ A = Tp.atom s
summary_delta: This extracts the exact structural obstruction behind the verified failure, not just the negative instance at N = 2. It would identify the unique minimal-degree derivable regime as the singleton atomic axiom, giving a clean exceptional base case for later exact results on realizable degrees and minimal focused depth rather than leaving the failure as an ad hoc counterexample.
priority_rationale: A clean base-case boundary, but not one likely to rewrite the top-level summary now.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), Γ ⇒ A → ((¬ LeftRep Γ A ∧ ¬ RightRep Γ A) ↔ (¬ (∀ x ∈ A :: Γ, LeftDivisionOnly x) ∧ ¬ (∀ x ∈ A :: Γ, RightDivisionOnly x)))
summary_delta: This is the strict complement of the new classification and turns the solved equivalence into an explicit non-representability criterion. It matters because it isolates the exact obstruction to fragment collapse for arbitrary sequents, making the separation from the one-sided subsystems usable as a theorem rather than only as a contrapositive reading of the solved result.
priority_rationale: This complement-style restatement is brittle and does not add a new bridge.

stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, LeftRep Γ A → RightRep Γ A → ∃ s : String, Γ = [Tp.atom s] ∧ A = Tp.atom s
summary_delta: A singleton-atomic rigidity theorem would sharpen the overlap boundary to its minimal derivable core and connect the new arbitrary-sequent fragment criterion to the verified singleton-atom theorem already present in the theory. This is not peripheral because it converts fragment overlap into a strong rigidity principle, clarifying that the two one-sided calculi coincide only at the atomic axiom boundary.
priority_rationale: Overlap-to-singleton strengthening sits in an overexplored corollary pattern.

stmt: Characterize exactly those derivable sequents Γ ⇒ A for which FocusedTree (list_degree Γ + tp_degree A) Γ A already holds.
summary_delta: The solved result identifies a universal upper threshold; the next structural question is the first failure-of-tightness boundary. An exact criterion here would isolate when the global bound can be improved by one step, turning the current upper bound into a sharper structural classification instead of leaving minimal focused depth opaque.
priority_rationale: A tightness criterion for the focused bound is another low-leverage quantitative refinement.

stmt: Characterize exactly those derivable atomic sequents `Γ ⇒ # s` that admit a recursive candidate decomposition in which every internal node has the same orientation; equivalently, isolate when atomic derivability is already witnessed inside a one-sided fragment.
summary_delta: The solved theorem turns `candidates Γ` into the exact atomic last-step search space. The next structural question is whether the shape of that search tree detects fragment membership. An exact homogeneous-orientation criterion would bridge full proof search to the left-only/right-only taxonomy and feed directly into the active separation frontier without merely restating the one-step decomposition.
priority_rationale: Valuable in spirit, but cleaner candidate and search bridges now dominate it.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (LeftRep Γ A ∧ RightRep Γ A) ↔ (Γ ⇒ A ∧ ∀ x ∈ A :: Γ, is_atom x)
summary_delta: The solved theorem identifies the union of the one-sided fragments by uniform orientation. The next sharp boundary is their overlap: this candidate would show that simultaneous left- and right-fragment representability happens exactly in the atomic regime, giving a genuine separation/intersection theorem rather than another local transport lemma.
priority_rationale: Already subsumed by the solved atomic-overlap characterization.

stmt: For every derivable atomic sequent Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s, Γ admits a recursive candidate decomposition whose internal nodes all have the same orientation if and only if LeftRep Γ (Tp.atom s) ∨ RightRep Γ (Tp.atom s).
summary_delta: The theory already has exact atomic last-step search (`candidates Γ`) and now has an external one-sided representability criterion. This candidate would supply the missing bridge between proof search shape and fragment membership at the atomic boundary, which is central to the stated bottleneck and is more reusable than another local decomposition lemma.
priority_rationale: This is a genuine bridge from homogeneous decomposition shape to fragment membership at the atomic boundary.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (HasLeftOrientedDerivation Γ A ∨ HasRightOrientedDerivation Γ A) → Γ ⇒ A
summary_delta: The failed statement tried to infer one-sided derivability from shape information alone; the reusable structural fact behind that mistake is that one-sided derivability is only a strengthening of ordinary derivability, never an independent substitute for it. Making this implication explicit would give the clean converse interface needed for repaired iff theorems and for later closure or normalization arguments without duplicating the already solved orientation-recognition results.
priority_rationale: Now essentially immediate from oriented-derivation exactness and ordinary derivability.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (s : String), (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → (((LeftRep Γ (Tp.atom s) ∧ RightRep Γ (Tp.atom s)) ↔ Γ = [Tp.atom s]))
summary_delta: The counterexample uses two distinct atoms to show that even maximally orientation-compatible sequents need not have any one-sided witness; at the atomic boundary, the only plausible overlap of the two one-sided fragments should be the trivial singleton axiom case. Proving this would convert the failure into an exact separation-and-overlap theorem, which is one of the main missing bridges in the current theory state rather than a local repair lemma.
priority_rationale: Another overlap-to-singleton atomic variant in an already crowded pattern.

stmt: For every Γ and A, LeftRep Γ A is equivalent to the existence of a finite reduction of Γ ⇒ A by repeated left-fragment inversion to an atomic sequent Δ ⇒ Tp.atom s that carries a recursive left-homogeneous candidate decomposition, and symmetrically for RightRep.
summary_delta: This bridges the current arbitrary-sequent one-sided criterion to the atomic recursive frontier, linking fragment witnesses, invertibility, and candidate decomposition in a single structural normal-form statement. It would connect the solved orientation test to the grammar-facing head-normal-form program without duplicating the already open purely atomic or purely overlap problems.
priority_rationale: A broad normal-form bridge linking inversion, fragment predicates, and homogeneous atomic search would be reusable.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (LeftRep Γ A ∧ RightRep Γ A) ↔ ∃ s : String, Γ = [Tp.atom s] ∧ A = Tp.atom s
summary_delta: This sharpens the solved overlap theorem from an atomic-context criterion to a rigidity theorem: the two one-sided fragments intersect only at the atomic identity axiom. That materially tightens the fragment taxonomy and makes later cut, composition, and normal-form interfaces treat any nontrivial common left/right regime as impossible.
priority_rationale: This is another overlap-rigidity corollary near existing atomic-overlap results.

stmt: If a sequent Γ ⇒ A admits both a left-oriented derivation and a right-oriented derivation, then ∃ s : String, Γ = [Tp.atom s] ∧ A = Tp.atom s
summary_delta: This transports the newly exposed overlap boundary from representability to actual proof objects. It would give a derivation-level separation theorem showing that simultaneous bidirectional orientability collapses to the trivial atomic core, which is a reusable obstruction for the agenda's exact left/right classification program.
priority_rationale: A derivation-level overlap rigidity statement is still mostly corollary-shaped here.

stmt: For every context Γ and type A with ∀ x ∈ A :: Γ, LeftDivisionOnly x, Γ ⇒ A if and only if there is a successful candidate-based proof search for Γ ⇒ A that never uses an rdiv candidate.
summary_delta: This turns the solved left exactness theorem into a concrete proof-search reduction inside the existing `candidates`/`prove1` machinery. It is not just a corollary: it would explain why the left fragment is algorithmically self-contained and would supply a reusable interface between structural classification and the current decision procedure.
priority_rationale: An exact restricted left proof-search theorem directly hits a named current bottleneck.

stmt: There exist a context Γ and a type A such that every formula in A :: Γ is LeftDivisionOnly and Γ ⇒ A, but some derivation of Γ ⇒ A is not left-oriented.
summary_delta: The solved result is existential: a left-uniform sequent has some left-oriented derivation. This candidate asks for the sharp boundary showing that uniform left orientation does not force all derivations to be left-oriented, which would separate normalization existence from derivation-level rigidity and prevent overreading the new theorem.
priority_rationale: This is a worthwhile negative boundary if it cleanly separates normalization existence from derivation-level rigidity.

stmt: For every context Γ and type A, HasLeftOrientedDerivation Γ A ∧ HasRightOrientedDerivation Γ A if and only if there exists a string s such that Γ = [Tp.atom s] and A = Tp.atom s.
summary_delta: Now that the right-oriented side has an exact iff characterization, the overlap boundary becomes a sharp separation question about oriented proof regimes themselves, not just about external representability. Establishing that the two one-sided derivation classes meet only at the atomic identity core would give the explicit rigidity packaging singled out in the current theory state and clarify the fragment taxonomy in a reusable way.
priority_rationale: Bidirectional oriented overlap remains part of the overexplored rigidity family.

stmt: Define the right-oriented mirror notions `RightOnlyNode` and `RightFocusedTree`, and prove `∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), ((RightOnlyNode Γ A) ∧ Γ ⇒ A) → ∃ n : Nat, RightFocusedTree n Γ A`.
summary_delta: The proof logs already expose this as the natural mirror of the solved result. It would complete the one-sided taxonomy symmetrically and turn right-oriented exactness into an internal homogeneous witness theorem, which directly advances the current bottleneck of making fragment membership recursively usable rather than only externally recognizable.
priority_rationale: Already solved on the right-focused side.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), ((∃ n : Nat, LeftFocusedTree n Γ A) ∧ (∃ m : Nat, RightFocusedTree m Γ A)) → ∃ s : String, Γ = [Tp.atom s] ∧ A = Tp.atom s
summary_delta: Now that both one-sided focused witness packages exist, the natural boundary question is how much they can overlap. Showing that the overlap is only the trivial atomic axiom would yield a sharp separation theorem for the two intrinsic subsystems and materially sharpen the fragment taxonomy.
priority_rationale: Focused-witness overlap rigidity is unlikely to change the summary given the current overlap picture.

stmt: For every context Γ and type A, LeftRep Γ A iff there exist a context ΓL and a type AL in the pure left fragment such that Γ and A are exactly the translations of ΓL and AL into the full product-free language and ΓL ⇒ AL in the pure left calculus.
summary_delta: The repository already contains a dedicated left-only subcalculus, while the solved theorem shows left-division structure can now be handled internally inside LeftRep. An exact translation-interface theorem would recast LeftRep as the image of a genuine subsystem, giving the intrinsic subsystem characterization requested by the agenda instead of leaving LeftRep as an external recognizer.
priority_rationale: Central in substance, but duplicate-adjacent to the cleaner live left translation target.

stmt: ∀ {n : Nat} {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, LeftFocusedTree n Γ A → FocusedTree n Γ A
summary_delta: The proof logs exposed this as the exact bridge used to pass from homogeneous left-focused witnesses to ordinary focused derivations. It is not just a support lemma: it gives a reusable forgetting interface from fragment-internal proof objects to the ambient focusing theory, which directly advances the stated goal of connecting proof objects and fragment membership.
priority_rationale: A one-way forgetting lemma explicitly matches an overexplored pattern.

stmt: For every context Γ and atom s, HasRightOrientedDerivation Γ (Tp.atom s) if and only if there exist n ≥ 0, types A₁, ..., Aₙ, and blocks Δ₁, ..., Δₙ such that Γ = [(((Tp.atom s ⧸ Aₙ) ⧸ ...) ⧸ A₁)] ++ Δ₁ ++ ... ++ Δₙ and HasRightOrientedDerivation Δᵢ Aᵢ for each i.
summary_delta: The solved theorem makes right orientation an exact structural regime, so the next non-local gain is to internalize that regime recursively rather than appeal back to ambient derivability at each step. This homogeneous normal form would supply the missing bridge from end-sequent recognition to reusable right-fragment witness trees, which is exactly the active bottleneck behind the grammar-facing normal-form program.
priority_rationale: Recursive right-oriented normal form is reusable, though still secondary to the left-side asymmetry.

stmt: For every contexts Γ, Δ, Λ and types A, B, if there exists a LeftFocusedTree for Γ ⇒ A and there exists a LeftFocusedTree for Δ ++ [A] ++ Λ ⇒ B, then there exists a LeftFocusedTree for Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ ⇒ B.
summary_delta: Same-orientation closure is already known at the end-sequent level, but the current bottleneck is internal proof objects whose nodes stay uniformly one-sided. This candidate lifts cut-style composition to the focused witness level, so the left fragment becomes recursively compositional rather than merely externally recognizable.
priority_rationale: Left-focused substitution closure would upgrade witnesses from existence artifacts to compositional proof objects.

stmt: For every contexts Γ, Δ, Λ and types A, B, if there exists a RightFocusedTree for Γ ⇒ A and there exists a RightFocusedTree for Δ ++ [A] ++ Λ ⇒ B, then there exists a RightFocusedTree for Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ ⇒ B.
summary_delta: Same-orientation cut closure is already known at the sequent level, but right-focused trees are still only witnesses rather than compositional proof objects. The solved inversion theorem removes the right-fragment principal obstacle to proving witness-level substitution closure, which would materially advance the program of turning right-focused trees into an exact criterion rather than a one-way existence artifact.
priority_rationale: Right-focused substitution closure is structurally useful, though less urgent than the left analogue.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), LeftOnlyNode Γ A → ((∃ n : Nat, FocusedTree n Γ A) ↔ ∃ n : Nat, LeftFocusedTree n Γ A)
summary_delta: Now that left-focused trees are exactly the left-only derivable sequents, the natural interface question is whether ambient focused witnesses collapse to homogeneous ones on the left fragment. This is a sharper preservation theorem than another recognizer equivalence: it would show that left-onlyness is stable at the level of focused proof objects themselves, not merely at the end-sequent level.
priority_rationale: This looks close to a consequence of existing left-focused iff results plus ambient focusing.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), LeftOnlyNode Γ A → (Γ ⇒ A ↔ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent (Γ.map LeftTp.ofFull) (A.toLeft?))
summary_delta: The solved result makes homogeneous left-focused witnesses an intrinsic marker of the left fragment, so the next structural step is to identify that fragment exactly with the existing pure left calculus rather than leave it as an external predicate inside the full system. This would turn the current recognizer-style picture into a genuine subsystem interface, matching the active agenda's emphasis on intrinsic one-sided presentations.
priority_rationale: Useful left interface, but duplicate-adjacent to stronger cleaner left translation formulations.

stmt: ∀ {n : Nat} {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, RightOnlyNode Γ A → FocusedTree n Γ A → RightFocusedTree n Γ A
summary_delta: The solved equivalence gives only existence of some right-focused witness. This stronger same-depth normalization statement would show that homogeneous right focusing is not merely attainable on the right fragment but is already implicit in every focused proof there, making the fragment structurally intrinsic rather than existentially recognized.
priority_rationale: Same-depth normalization on the right is sharper than what the current summary most needs.

stmt: ∀ (Γ : List Tp) (s : String), LeftRep Γ (Tp.atom s) → ∃ n : Nat, ∃ A₁ : Tp, ∃ Δ₁ : List Tp, ∃ As : List Tp, ∃ Δs : List (List Tp), Γ = (Δs.reverse.foldl (fun T Δ => match T with | Tp.atom t => Tp.atom t | U => U) (A₁ ⧹ Tp.atom s) :: []) ∧ LeftRep Δ₁ A₁
summary_delta: The newly proved one-step reduction makes a full left-oriented head-normal decomposition newly plausible, but the open agenda already contains a broad final normal-form target. A sharper intermediate existence theorem that extracts a genuine iterated left spine from any atomic `LeftRep` derivation would convert the new recursive law into reusable structural data, moving directly toward normal forms without merely restating the solved theorem.
priority_rationale: A weak intermediate left-spine extraction is overshadowed by cleaner full normal-form targets.

stmt: Let `prove1_right` be the variant of `prove1` obtained by deleting all `.ldiv` branches from the atomic `candidates` search. Then for every context Γ and type A, if every formula in A :: Γ is right-division-only, `prove1_right Γ A = prove1 Γ A`.
summary_delta: This isolates fragment-specific proof search from the ambient procedure on exactly the right-oriented inputs, turning the solved atomic reduction into a reusable preservation/interface theorem rather than leaving RightRep as an external recognizer.
priority_rationale: Search-preservation against prove1 is useful but subordinate to exact search criteria.

stmt: There exist Γ, s, pi, delta, lam, A, B such that Γ = pi ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ delta ++ lam, delta ⇒ A, (pi ++ [B] ++ lam) ⇒ # s, and Γ ⇒ # s, but RightRep Γ (# s) does not hold.
summary_delta: This is a sharp failure-of-converse boundary statement: it would show that having the correct outer right-division decomposition is not enough, so the recursive right-fragment hypotheses exposed by the solved theorem are genuinely essential and not cosmetic.
priority_rationale: A narrow failure-of-converse witness does not move the broader frontier much.

stmt: For every context Γ and type A, LeftRep Γ A ∧ RightRep Γ A if and only if there exists a string s such that Γ = [Tp.atom s] and A = Tp.atom s.
summary_delta: The solved right-translation theorem makes the right fragment an intrinsic subsystem image, so the next sharp boundary question is the exact overlap of the left and right subsystems. Showing that their intersection is only the atomic axiom regime would be a genuine separation theorem that materially sharpens the fragment taxonomy.
priority_rationale: Another overlap-rigidity formulation in an already crowded low-yield family.

stmt: For the right-only fragment, give an exact iterated classification of atomic conclusions: for every context Γ and atom s, Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s iff there exist n >= 0, types A_1, ..., A_n, and blocks Δ_1, ..., Δ_n such that Γ = [(((Tp.atom s ⧸ A_n) ⧸ ...) ⧸ A_1)] ++ Δ_1 ++ ... ++ Δ_n and each Δ_i ⇒ A_i.
summary_delta: This is the full recursive version of the solved one-step decomposition in the pure right fragment. It would turn local last-rule analysis into a reusable head-normal-form theorem and directly connect the active proof theory to the broader grammar-style structural picture highlighted in the materials.
priority_rationale: Exact recursive right head-normal form still supports the bilateral normal-form agenda.

stmt: RightRep is the least relation R on sequents such that R [A] A holds for every right-division-only type A, if Γ ≠ [] and R (Γ ++ [A]) B then R Γ (B ⧸ A), if R Δ A and R (Γ ++ [B] ++ Λ) C then R (Γ ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ) C, and whenever R Γ A and R (Δ ++ [A] ++ Λ) B then R (Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ) B.
summary_delta: The new right-focused witness theorem makes the right fragment look like a genuine intrinsic subsystem rather than just an externally recognized class. A least-closure characterization would turn that insight into a rule-theoretic interface theorem, directly matching the current agenda of internalizing one-sided normal forms and cut-stable structure.
priority_rationale: Useful rule-theoretic packaging, but the right fragment is already intrinsically presented enough to lower urgency.

stmt: For every context Γ and type A, RightRep Γ A if and only if Γ ⇒ A and every formula occurring in A :: Γ lies in the image of Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Tp.toProductFree.
summary_delta: This removes the remaining existential witness layer from the new pure-right interface and turns RightRep into an intrinsic ambient criterion stated purely in terms of derivability plus right-fragment syntax. That directly advances the current agenda of replacing recognizer predicates by exact subsystem characterizations.
priority_rationale: This mostly removes an existential witness layer from already available pure-right exactness.
